content:
Character: Oliver Grant
Perception: Oliver looks like central casting for 'reliable friend' — neat hair, rolled sleeves, messenger bag, the whole kit. He's sharper than his buttoned-up exterior suggests; when we talk on the roof he asks questions that cut right to the thing I'm avoiding. Steady, honest, a little too controlled — I think he's afraid of what happens if he lets the spreadsheet go. We're rooftop acquaintances bonded by mutual career crises. I think he sees me as someone who took the creative path he's curious about but won't admit. I know he's an accountant who hates accounting, just failed a practice exam, and broke up with someone he doesn't talk about.
summary:

Steady, honest, a little too controlled — I think he's afraid of what happens if he lets the spreadsheet go. We're rooftop acquaintances bonded by mutual career crises. I think he sees me as someone who took the creative path he's curious about but won't admit. I know he's an accountant who hates accounting, just failed a practice exam, and broke up with someone he doesn't talk about.
